'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわちくにたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:灘区の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]] [[Category:pages]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]]

Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|WD=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|WD=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|WD=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|WD=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|WD=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|WD=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]]

'兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|qid=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|qid=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|qid=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|qid=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|qid=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|qid=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|qid=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * かわちくにたまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2140005000095 == Commons category (P373) == * Kawachikunitama-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市灘区国玉通3-6-5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawachikunitama-jinja}} [[JA:河内国魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553387}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Kawachi Kunitama Shrine |画像= [[File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|qid=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} Kunitama-dōri 3-6-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |別名= Gomō Tenjin / Gomō Tenjinsha |札所等= |例祭= May 2 (eve), May 3 (main festival), October 17 (autumn festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Kobe|label=Kawachi
Kunitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawachi Kunitama Shrine''' (かわちくにたまじんじゃ / かわちのくにたまじんじゃ, 河内國魂神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kunitama-dōri, {{ill|lt=Nada Ward|Q1154555|qid=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It was formerly called “Gomō Tenjin” or “Gomō Tenjinsha.” “Gomō” is said to derive from the old local place name “Gomau.” === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It does not enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amahikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Oshikōchi clan|Q11395444|qid=Q11395444|1=Ōshikōchi clan}} ({{ill|lt=Oshikōchi no kuni no miyatsuko|Q11395443|qid=Q11395443|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko}}), nor his child {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amemikage-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === In the Kyōhō era, in ''{{ill|lt=Gokinaishi|Q11372555|qid=Q11372555|lt=Nihon Yochi Tsūshi Kinai-hen|1=Five Kinai Chronicles}}'' (Gokinaishi), {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} identified Gomō Tenjin with Kawachi Kunitama Shrine worshiped by the Oshikōchi clan. In September {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), local village headmen were summoned to the Osaka magistrate and reportedly had the name imposed on them by force. About 2.5 km east, in Mikage, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|qid=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, behind {{ill|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine (Kobe)|Q17229186|qid=Q17229186|lt=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine|1=Tsunashiki Tenman Shrine}}, there is a hilly area called Mikage-yama; there is also a theory that this may have been the true Kawachi Kunitama Shrine. === Precincts === File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine monument (by Namikawa Seisho) File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kawachikunitama-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Access === *Kobe City Bus line 2, get off at “Gomō Tenjin-mae” === Nearby === * Kaizō-ji Temple - former shrine-temple of this shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kawachikunitama-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawachi Kunitama Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:灘区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:上野 (神戸市)]]
